title: Handle Missing Snowflake Semantic Views During Metrics Import
story: As a DataTools Pro user, I want to be notified when no Snowflake semantic views are available during Metrics Analyst import, so that I can trigger a refresh or navigate to the semantic views page to set one up.
description:
When a user attempts to import Snowflake data via the Metrics tab and no semantic views are available, the system should handle this gracefully.

### Options
1. **In-context refresh** — Notify the user and either auto-trigger a semantic view refresh or provide a button to trigger one from the metrics import screen
2. **Redirect** — If adding refresh logic to the import screen is complex, redirect the user to the Semantic Views page where refresh is already available

The preferred approach depends on implementation complexity.
acceptance_criteria: User is notified when no Snowflake semantic views are available during import
User can trigger a semantic view refresh or is redirected to the Semantic Views page
After refresh completes, the import flow can proceed with the now-available semantic views
ux_changes: Notification or empty-state message when no semantic views are found during Metrics Analyst import. Option to trigger refresh inline or navigate to Semantic Views page.
middleware_changes: Check for available semantic views before import. Optional: trigger semantic view refresh from the metrics import context.
implementation_notes: Implemented on dev and ready for review. Approach chosen was to handle the empty state with notification and refresh/redirect option.
comment_summary: Waqar implemented the feature on dev and marked it ready for review.
